title: Speed Clip Strapping
content_markdown:
#### Indications

- To secure a patient to a spine board or scoop stretcher.
- Patient extrication.

#### Procedure

- Place the patient on the spine board with shoulders just covering the second set of handholds.

- Place adequate padding under the lumbar spine and head to fill gaps caused by anatomical curvature of the spine.

- Apply first strap across the chest with the adjustable end at the pelvis. Tighten strap enough to allow one hand to be placed between the chest and the strap. The strap should be placed over the clavicle and attached to the pelvic handhold.

- Apply the second strap across the other shoulder in the same manner as in step 3. The first two straps prevent sliding of the patient’s body toward the head and lateral movement of the torso.

- Apply a third strap as high up as possible across the chest from left to right. If the strap is applied too low across the chest, chest expansion during respiration may be compromised. This will prevent lateral movement.

- Place a strap across the pelvic bone or iliac crest. Ensure the strap goes over the bone rather than the soft abdomen.

- Place a strap across the femurs. Utilise additional padding as required.

- Apply a figure of eight around the patients ankles to prevent downward sliding of the patient on the board.

- One further strap can be placed loosely over the chest region to secure the patient’s arms.

#### Complications

- Inadequately secured patient can aggravate spinal injuries.
